English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
W. Sterling Cole papers
Cole, W. Sterling (William Sterling), 1904-1987.
Morrissey, Charles T.
2081
42 cubic feet. 42 cubic feet.
[ca. 1935-1957] Correspondence, Manuscripts, Printed Materials, Publications. Representative Cole's papers, which pertain almost exclusively to his years in the U.S. House of Representatives. Collection material in English 2100957
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
S. Deskis
Date completed:
March 1985
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, November 2008
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, November 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Congressman, New York 37th District, 1935-1944, 1953-1957, 39th District, 1945-1952; Director General, International Atomic Energy Agency, Vienna, 1957-1961.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Representative Cole's papers, which pertain almost exclusively to his years in the U.S. House of Representatives, include correspondence, copies of proposed bills, and newspaper clippings concerning the Joint Committee on Atomic Energy, the Committee on Armed Services, and the Naval Affairs Committee; numerous letters from constituents expressing opinions on specific legislation, presidential appointments, foreign affairs, and other issues and letters concerning pensions, workmen's compensation and other claims, appointments to the U.S. Military, Naval, and Air Force Academies, and immigration cases. Also, correspondence with the Republican Party organization and individuals concerning Congressional district campaigns and elections; letters to and from constituents and local officails pertaining to federal reimbursements for damages suffered in the 1935 flood, and inquiries and criticisms regarding flood control proposals of the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers; pamphlets issued by special interest groups; mimeographed newsletters of local Republican groups, printed campaign materials, and voters' lists; and personal papers and farm accounts, Bath, Steuben County.
Also, 37 pp. transcript of oral history interview with W. Sterling Cole, conducted by Charles T. Morrissey, August 24, 1978.
Restrictions on Use:
Publication of quotations from the oral history interview requires consent of the U.S. Association of Former Members of Congress
Cite As:
W. Sterling Cole papers, #2081, Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Oral histories. Steuben County (N.Y.) -- Politics and government. New York (State) -- Politics and government. United States -- Politics and government. Politicians. Farm management -- New York (State) -- Steuben County. Agriculture -- Accounting. Flood control -- New York (State) Floods -- New York (State) Politics, Practical. Elections -- New York (State) Elections -- United States. Political campaigns. Emigration and immigration law -- United States. Workers\' compensation -- United States. Military pensions. Survivors\' benefits. Old age pensions -- Law and legislation. Veterans\' -- Medical care. Cole, W. Sterling (William Sterling), 1904-1987. United States. Congress. House United States. Congress. Joint Committee on Atomic Energy United States. Congress. House. Committee on Armed Services United States. Congress. House. Committee on Naval Affairs United States Military Academy United States Naval Academy United States Air Force Academy Republican Party (U.S. : 1854- ) Republican Party (N.Y.) United States. Army. Corps of Engineers
